attacking_style: Opportunistic attacks targeting alternative objectives (KOM points, positioning) rather than pure stage wins. Attacks deployed across multiple laps/climbs when outright victory appears unlikely.
team_tactics: Flexible resource allocation based on race situation: commits full domestique depth (including GC riders) for protected rider positioning, prioritizes GC support over sprint opportunities when hierarchy demands.
terrain_preference: Circuit races with multiple climbing opportunities for repeated attacks; final climbs where deep domestique support can deliver tactical advantage for positioning or sprint leadouts.
vulnerability: Appears to lack outright firepower for stage wins on difficult terrain, forcing pivot to secondary objectives. Mechanical incidents trigger resource-intensive recovery operations that expose team priorities.
key_patterns: Multiple riders attack for KOM points when stage win unlikely (2 observations)
Uses GC-caliber domestiques for leadout/positioning work, not reserved for pure GC support (2 observations)
Commits disproportionate resources to recover/protect designated leaders from setbacks (2 observations)
Tactical flexibility: shifts objectives mid-race based onwin probability assessment (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 data shows continued opportunistic approach without clear performance progression from 2022 patterns
